methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Strand Therapeutics is a Series B-stage biotech company founded by Jake Becraft and Tasuku Kitada. The company has raised $205M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private peers in Biotech & Health plus 3 public-market reference points (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods apply to Strand's valuation. Stage-progression methodology, anchoring on the $205M total raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $390M. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same-stage companies—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yields a median valuation of $810M, with a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 6.6x across the three public biotech names) cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures, which private companies typically do not provide.

Synthesizing these two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $331M (low) to $931M (high), with a median estimate of $600M. The 3x spread between methods reflects material uncertainty in peer selection weighting and stage-specific growth assumptions. Key unknowns include clinical-stage progress, intellectual property quality, and burn rate relative to runway—factors not quantified in comparable data.
